devotion.title: The Power of Forgiving the Unforgivable
devotion.scripture: Colossians 3:13 (NIV) - Bear with each other and forgive one another if any of you has a grievance against someone. Forgive as the Lord forgave you.
devotion.story:
In 1947, Corrie ten Boom, a Dutch Christian who survived the Ravensbrück concentration camp during World War II, faced an impossible moment. She was speaking in a Munich church about God’s forgiveness when she saw him—the former SS guard who had stood guard over the showers where her sister Betsie died. The man approached, hand outstretched, saying, ‘How grateful I am for your message. To think that, as you say, He has washed my sins away!’

Corrie froze. For years she had prayed for the strength to forgive, but in that instant, hatred and anger flooded her heart. She knew that forgiveness is an act of the will, not a feeling. As the man stood waiting, she cried out silently: ‘Lord, I cannot forgive him. Give me Your forgiveness.’

Then, as she later wrote, a supernatural warmth flooded her. She took his hand, and with all her heart she said, ‘I forgive you, brother.’ They stood there, hands clasped, former enemies reconciled.

Corrie discovered that forgiveness is not about excusing the inexcusable, but about releasing the offender into God’s justice and mercy. She learned that we forgive not because the other person deserves it, but because Christ has already forgiven us far more. That day, a guard who had caused unspeakable pain was set free—and so was she.

Forgiveness does not mean forgetting; it means remembering without the poison of bitterness. When we forgive as we have been forgiven, we break the chains of the past and step into the freedom of God’s grace.
